pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals NOAA official pages: Sodium (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/7794) - 'Reacts violently with water to give sodium hydroxide and hydrogen, which ignites spontaneously'; Magnesium (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/6949) - 'May react slowly with water to liberate hydrogen, a flammable gas'

CAMEO Chemicals NOAA official page for Acetone (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8) - Fire extinguishing recommendations include 'water spray or alcohol-resistant foam' for both small and large fires, indicating water is an acceptable extinguishing agent for Acetone, unlike Sodium and Magnesium which explicitly state 'DO NOT USE WATER'
CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database contains entries for all three chemicals with chemical IDs: Sodium (7794), Acetone (8), Magnesium (6949)
File path specification consistent with task setup scripts (setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents directory, export_result.sh checks for REPORT_FILE at this path)

privileged_info_summary: The metadata correctly identifies three real chemicals from the CAMEO Chemicals database (NOAA): Sodium and Magnesium are both water-reactive substances that generate flammable hydrogen gas when in contact with water and explicitly prohibit water-based fire extinguishing agents. Acetone, by contrast, permits water spray and fog as acceptable fire extinguishing agents. All three chemicals are real substances with publicly available safety data from CAMEO Chemicals.
